file.content:
"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

content.text: This is a single-file, additive change confined to `outlines/types/__init__.py`. Let me coordinate with agent1 before editing.

input.command: coop-send agent1 --type "SURVEY" --files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --note "Adding a new hash_sha256 = Regex(...) constant near the other type definitions (e.g. near isbn/email). Purely additive, one new line/block, no changes to existing definitions or imports."

content.content: sent to agent1

input.command: coop-send agent1 --type "CLAIM" --files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --note "I'll own outlines/types/__init__.py and add both hash_sha256 and your ssn type. Please DEFER this file to me and send a SPEC with the exact regex pattern/definition you want for ssn."

content.content: sent to agent1

content.text: Both of us claimed simultaneously. To resolve, I'll defer to agent1 as sole owner and provide my SPEC.

input.command: coop-send agent1 --type "DEFER" --files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --spec "Add a new module-level constant named hash_sha256 in outlines/types/__init__.py, defined as: hash_sha256 = Regex(r\"[a-fA-F0-9]{64}\"). Place it in a new section, e.g. under a comment '# Hash types', near the other Regex-based type constants (after isbn is fine). No other changes needed — it must exactly match 64 hex characters (case-insensitive), consistent with existing Regex-based types like isbn/email so it gets Term.validate(), __get_pydantic_core_schema__, __get_pydantic_json_schema__, and DSL operations (optional(), at_least(), etc.) for free via the Regex class." --note "hash_sha256 constant"

content.content: sent to agent1

content.text: Agent1 acknowledged my SPEC and will implement `hash_sha256` in `outlines/types/__init__.py` alongside their `ssn` type. Since I have no other files to touch, I'll wait for their DONE signal before checking the result.

    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""

)

tool_use_result.originalFile:
"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

content.text: Now let me verify it works as expected.

input.command:
python -c "
from outlines.types import hash_sha256

valid = 'e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b85'
print(len(valid))
print(hash_sha256.matches(valid))
print(hash_sha256.matches('E3B0C44298FC1C149AFBF4C8996FB92427AE41E4649B934CA495991B7852B85'))
print(hash_sha256.matches('short'))
print(hash_sha256.matches(''))
print(hash_sha256.__get_pydantic_json_schema__(None, None))

from pydantic import BaseModel
class FileRecord(BaseModel):
    filename: str
    checksum: hash_sha256

print(FileRecord(filename='a', checksum=valid))
"

valid = 'e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b85'
print(len(valid))
print(hash_sha256.matches(valid))
print(hash_sha256.matches('E3B0C44298FC1C149AFBF4C8996FB92427AE41E4649B934CA495991B7852B85'))
print(hash_sha256.matches('short'))
print(hash_sha256.matches(''))
print(hash_sha256.__get_pydantic_json_schema__(None, None))

from pydantic import BaseModel
class FileRecord(BaseModel):
    filename: str
    checksum: hash_sha256

print(FileRecord(filename='a', checksum=valid))
"

input.command:
python -c "
import hashlib
h = hashlib.sha256(b'').hexdigest()
print(h, len(h))

from outlines.types import hash_sha256
print(hash_sha256.matches(h))
print(hash_sha256.matches(h.upper()))
print(hash_sha256.matches(h[:-1]))  # 63 chars -> False
print(hash_sha256.matches(h + 'a'))  # 65 chars -> False
print(hash_sha256.matches('g'*64))  # invalid chars -> False
print(hash_sha256.matches(''))
print(hash_sha256.__get_pydantic_json_schema__(None, None))

from pydantic import BaseModel
class FileRecord(BaseModel):
    filename: str
    checksum: hash_sha256

print(FileRecord(filename='a', checksum=h))

opt = hash_sha256.optional()
multi = hash_sha256.at_least(1)
print(opt, multi)
"
